title: Cloudflare Bot Management Score Distribution and Challenge Pass Rate (`cloudflare:json`)
dataSources: Cloudflare Logpush JSON delivered to Splunk HEC/S3 with `sourcetype=cloudflare:json` (HTTP requests, firewall, Workers, DNS, Access as configured)
app: Cloudflare App for Splunk (Splunkbase 4501) + Logpush JSON (`cloudflare:json`)
spl:
index=cdn sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-24h
| spath BotScore
| spath BotScoreSrc
| bin BotScore span=10
| stats count by BotScore
| eventstats sum(count) as tot
| eval pct=round(100*count/tot,2)
| sort BotScore
description: Bot scores quantify automated abuse versus humans. A collapsing median score or falling challenge pass rates indicates credential stuffing or scraping.
value: Application security teams quantify the bot-versus-human traffic mix across protected domains, detect credential-stuffing surges on login endpoints, and validate that Bot Management challenges are stopping automated abuse without impacting legitimate partner integrations.
implementation: Configure Logpush jobs per dataset; route all into a unified `cloudflare:json` sourcetype or use transforms to normalize. Use `spath` for PascalCase fields. Create macros per zone name.
visualization: Histogram (BotScore), line chart (median score), single value (automated request %).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Web.Web
  by Web.user, Web.url, Web.http_method span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cloudflare App for Splunk (Splunkbase 4501) v2.x on the Search Head. Cloudflare Logpush configured for the **HTTP Requests** dataset with Bot Management fields enabled.
• Cloudflare Bot Management add-on (Enterprise plan required). The Logpush HTTP Requests dataset must include: `BotScore`, `BotScoreSrc` (machine_learning, heuristics, js_fingerprinting, behavioral_analysis), `BotTags`, `ClientIP`, `ClientRequestHost`, `ClientRequestURI`, `EdgeResponseStatus`.
• Data routed to `index=cdn` (or `index=waf`) with `sourcetype=cloudflare:json`.
• Bot Management is an Enterprise add-on — without it, `BotScore` is absent from Logpush. Verify your Cloudflare contract includes Bot Management.
• Create a `bot_partners.csv` lookup: `ip_range,partner_name,expected_bot_score` for known good bots (Googlebot, monitoring services, payment webhooks).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ensure the Logpush job for HTTP Requests includes Bot Management fields. In Cloudflare: Analytics & Logs → Logpush → edit the HTTP Requests job → confirm `BotScore` and `BotScoreSrc` are in the selected fields.

Verify:
```spl
index=cdn sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-15m
| spath BotScore | spath BotScoreSrc
| where isnotnull(BotScore)
| stats count by BotScoreSrc
```
You should see sources like `machine_learning`, `heuristics`, `js_fingerprinting`. If `BotScore` is always null, your Cloudflare plan may not include Bot Management, or the field was not selected in the Logpush job configuration.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Bot score distribution:
```spl
index=cdn sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-24h
| spath BotScore | spath BotScoreSrc | spath ClientRequestHost
| where isnotnull(BotScore)
| eval bot_band=case(BotScore<=1, "Verified Bot", BotScore<=10, "Very Likely Bot (2-10)", BotScore<=29, "Likely Bot (11-29)", BotScore<=49, "Suspicious (30-49)", BotScore>=50, "Likely Human (50-99)", 1==1, "Unknown")
| stats count as requests dc(ClientIP) as unique_ips by bot_band, BotScoreSrc
| sort bot_band
```

Understanding this SPL: Cloudflare's BotScore ranges 1-99, where 1 = definitely automated and 99 = definitely human. Score 0 indicates verified good bots (Googlebot). Banding the scores reveals the traffic mix. A shift toward lower bands (more bots) on login or checkout endpoints signals credential stuffing or scraping campaigns. The `BotScoreSrc` shows which detection engine classified the request — `machine_learning` is most reliable; `heuristics` may have higher false-positive rates.

Challenge pass rate:
```spl
index=cdn sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-24h
| spath BotScore | spath EdgeResponseStatus | spath ClientRequestHost
| where BotScore<30
| eval challenged=if(EdgeResponseStatus=403 OR match(_raw,"challenge"), 1, 0)
| stats count as total sum(challenged) as challenges by ClientRequestHost
| eval challenge_rate=round(challenges/total*100, 1)
| sort - total
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Cloudflare dashboard: Security → Bots → Bot Analytics. Compare the score distribution chart with your Splunk `bot_band` breakdown for the same time range.
(b) Pick a known good bot IP (e.g., Googlebot from `66.249.x.x`), verify it shows `BotScore=0` or very low with `BotScoreSrc=verified_bot`.
(c) Pick a known human session (your own IP), verify `BotScore>=80`.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard ("Cloudflare — Bot Management"):
• Row 1 — Pie chart: traffic by bot_band. Single-value: "% traffic from likely bots (score < 30)".
• Row 2 — Timechart: requests by bot_band over 7 days to spot trend shifts.
• Row 3 — Table: top 20 IPs with BotScore < 10, showing request count, target hosts, and paths.

Alert: Trigger when the ratio of BotScore < 30 requests to total requests exceeds 40% on any single `ClientRequestHost` over a 1h window. This catches sudden bot surges against specific applications.

Runbook:
1. **Sudden surge of low-score traffic on login endpoint** — Likely credential stuffing. Enable Cloudflare's Bot Fight Mode or create a firewall rule to challenge requests with BotScore < 30 on `/login` paths.
2. **Partner API returning high bot scores** — Add the partner's IP ranges to a Cloudflare verified bot list or create a WAF exception. Update `bot_partners.csv`.
3. **Gradual increase in "Suspicious" band** — Bots are evolving their fingerprints. Review `BotScoreSrc` — if mostly `heuristics`, the ML model may need retraining (Cloudflare-side).

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **BotScore always null** — Bot Management is not enabled on your Cloudflare plan, or the Logpush job was configured before Bot Management was activated. Recreate the Logpush job to pick up the new fields.
• **All traffic shows BotScore=99** — Logpush may be delivering sampled data. Verify the Logpush job is set to 100% sample rate. Also check if a Cloudflare Page Rule is bypassing Bot Management for the zone.
• **Discrepancy between Cloudflare Bot Analytics and Splunk** — Bot Analytics uses real-time sampled data; Logpush delivers full unsampled logs. Splunk numbers are authoritative for volume; Cloudflare dashboard is better for real-time trends.
grandmaExplanation: We count how much of our web traffic comes from real people versus automated programs, so we can block the bad bots while letting the helpful ones through.
